text: convenience: In edit mode: make description text area so large that shows all lines of the initially loaded description.
description: Don't update height dynamically (once on modal mount/data available). On create aim, current height is fine.

reflections.context: Implement a small UI convenience for edit-mode aims: make the description textarea tall enough for the initially loaded description, without changing create mode or adding dynamic autoresize behavior.
reflections.outcome: Added an initial row count in AimEditModal based on the loaded description line count with a three-row minimum, bound only the edit modal description textarea to that value, and added regression tests for multiline initial sizing, no dynamic resizing after user edits, and default height for short descriptions.
reflections.effectiveness: The change stayed localized to AimEditModal and its test file, matching the aim scope. Focused tests, frontend type-check, and the full frontend unit suite all pass.
reflections.lesson: For one-shot UI sizing, derive state at modal-open/data-load time rather than watching the input value; this makes the absence of dynamic resizing explicit and testable.
reflections.pattern: Small modal ergonomics are safest when covered with tests that assert both the intended behavior and the explicitly excluded behavior.

status.comment: Implemented edit-mode-only initial description textarea row sizing in AimEditModal. Multiline loaded descriptions now set the initial rows to fit all loaded lines, short descriptions keep the default height, and later edits do not dynamically resize. Verified with focused AimEditModal tests, frontend type-check, and full frontend unit suite.
